forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over SA will remain dominant as it slowly moves east and into the Tasman Sea on Friday. A cold front move across across South Australia over the weekend, followed by another high pressure system.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.
Morning fog patches about the agricultural areas. Mostly dry. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Morning fog patches about the east and southeast. Isolated showers developing about the west during the day. Maximum temperatures near to below average. East to northeasterly winds, fresh at times about the west.

Isolated showers in the west, extending into central parts during the afternoon and possibly reaching eastern parts in the evening. Showers becoming scattered about western coasts during the afternoon. Possible thunderstorms about the far west. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Northeast to northwesterly winds, with a south to southeasterly change moving across western districts during the day, fresh at times near the coast.

text.p: Isolated showers extending statewide, tending scattered about the southern agricultural area and western coasts. Maximum temperatures below average. North to northwesterly winds, ahead of a south to southeasterly change about the west moving across remaining districts during the day, fresh at times near the coast.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Sunday: less than 2 mm about central and eastern districts, 1 to 5 mm about southern coasts and ranges and the west. Local falls of 5 to 15 mm about western coasts.
